---
title: "Single-cell RNA-seq — Cell-type Report"
subtitle: "Sample deliverable · 10x pbmc3k"
author: "Peter Ristow, PhD — Principal Scientist (NGS)"
date: today
format:
  typst:
    papersize: a4
    margin:
      x: 1.8cm
      y: 1.8cm
    fontsize: 10pt
execute:
  echo: false
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
import pandas as pd, scanpy as sc, matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from IPython.display import Markdown
comp = pd.read_csv("results/celltype_composition.csv")
adata = sc.read_h5ad("results/pbmc3k_annotated.h5ad")
n_cells_s = f"{adata.n_obs:,}"
n_types_s = str(adata.obs["cell_type"].nunique())
```

**Executive summary.** A raw 10x single-cell matrix was taken end-to-end — quality control,
normalisation, clustering and **marker-based cell-type annotation** — to a labelled map of the
immune populations present. **`{python} n_cells_s` quality-controlled cells** resolved into
**`{python} n_types_s` annotated cell types**, each validated by canonical markers.

### Cell-type composition

```{python}
_hdr = "| " + " | ".join(comp.columns) + " |"
_sep = "| " + " | ".join(["---"] * len(comp.columns)) + " |"
_rows = ["| " + " | ".join(str(v) for v in row) + " |" for row in comp.values]
Markdown("\n".join([_hdr, _sep, *_rows]))
```

```{python}
#| fig-width: 5.4
#| fig-height: 3.4
sc.pl.umap(adata, color="cell_type", legend_loc="right margin",
           title="Annotated PBMC cell types", show=False, size=12)
plt.tight_layout(); plt.show()
```

**What this means.** The clusters are *biologically interpretable* — T cells, monocyte subsets, B,
NK, dendritic and a small megakaryocyte population — not merely statistically distinct. That
internal consistency is the real quality signal.

**Caveats.** Cluster resolution is a question-driven choice; CD8 T cells sit adjacent to CD4 T at
this depth and are honestly flagged rather than forced apart. Multi-sample work additionally needs
doublet detection and batch integration before cross-condition comparison.

**Reproducibility.** The pipeline re-runs from a clean clone in minutes and is verified by
continuous integration. Code and live report: `github.com/pgristow/scrnaseq-pbmc-scanpy`.

---
*Prepared by Peter Ristow, PhD — Roche Principal Scientist (NGS). Production engagements use a
private, hardened pipeline on your data under NDA.*
